access.user: Can upload in the comment attachment flow; download permissions must be re-validated.
access.admin: No standalone public 'upload management console' currently exists.
access.agent: Can list, rename, and delete the current user's upload metadata through MCP; binary object transfer remains REST/HTTP-only.

rules.comment-attachments-only: Uploads serve only comment attachments; they are not an independent file space.
rules.permission-and-quota: Permission and quota checks are required before upload.
rules.three-step-upload: Web comment attachment uploads follow a three-step flow — create upload session on-site → browser PUT to an on-site Cloudflare Workers upload object route backed by R2 → confirm completion on-site.
rules.r2-backend: Upload object create/read/head/delete operations use the Cloudflare R2 binding. The app runtime does not use S3-compatible signed URL fallbacks.
rules.storage-observability: Shared storage helpers record production-safe bounded metrics for storage operation status and duration; metrics identify operation type but do not include bucket names, object keys, signed URLs, filenames, or user IDs.
rules.safe-filenames: Upload create, completion, and rename requests reject ASCII control characters in filenames; download responses use a safe Content-Disposition filename fallback with an encoded UTF-8 filename parameter.
rules.e2e-r2: E2E upload coverage should exercise the same Workers upload object route used in production.
rules.one-upload-one-comment: A completed upload can be attached to at most one comment. Reusing an upload id across comments is rejected instead of treated as implicit file sharing.
rules.download-auth-required: Downloads must not bypass authorization by obtaining a direct link.
rules.download-delivery: /api/uploads/[id]/download validates owner access or visible comment-attachment access before streaming the object from the R2 binding.
rules.mcp-transfer-boundary: MCP upload tools manage upload metadata and ownership actions only. Creating upload sessions, PUT object transfer, completion, and download delivery remain REST/HTTP surfaces because they depend on browser/client byte streams and R2 object responses rather than text-formatted JSON tool output.

notes: The same path currently handles both 'read upload quota / history' and 'create upload session' capabilities.

tools.name: list_my_uploads
notes: Lists completed uploads and quota state for the current user; does not create upload sessions or stream object bytes.

routes.returns: { key: String, url: String, maxFileSizeBytes: Int, quotaBytes: Int, usedBytes: Int }
notes: The returned upload URL points to the on-site Workers upload object route and writes through the R2 binding.
Writes the pending object to R2 after re-validating auth, key ownership, reservation expiry, and size limits.

routes.returns: { upload: Upload, usedBytes: Int, quotaBytes: Int }

notes: Validates owner access or access through a visible attached comment before serving the object from R2.
Renames one upload owned by the current user.

Deletes one upload owned by the current user. The backing storage object is deleted before metadata is removed; storage cleanup failure returns an error and leaves the upload metadata available for retry.

tools.name: rename_my_upload
notes: Requires an unsuspended signed-in user and only renames uploads owned by the current user.

tools.name: delete_my_upload
tools.returns: { success: Boolean, deletedId: String, deletedSize: Int }
notes: Requires an unsuspended signed-in user, deletes only uploads owned by the current user, removes the backing storage object before metadata deletion, returns storage_delete_failed without deleting metadata when storage cleanup fails, and commits metadata deletion plus the upload_delete audit log together after successful storage deletion.
